nativeRange.habitat: Seasonal wetlands, marshes, and riverbanks in tropical Africa

decorPreferences: open background placement with room to grow tall
bright lighting to develop golden coloration

summary: The Golden Nesea is a background stem plant from tropical Africa, prized for the warm golden-yellow foliage and pink-to-red stems it develops under high light, CO2 injection, and iron-rich fertilization. It is considered intermediate in difficulty and best suited to high-tech planted aquariums.
careNotes:
Golden Nesea is the aquarium trade name for Nesaea pedicellata 'Golden', a stem plant native to seasonal wetlands, marshes, and riverbanks across tropical West and southern Africa. The older scientific name Nesaea pedicellata is still widely used in the hobby, though a 2013 revision of the family Lythraceae folded the entire genus Nesaea into Ammannia, meaning the plant is increasingly listed as Ammannia pedicellata 'Golden'.

Submersed stems typically reach 20 to 40 cm in height, making Golden Nesea most useful as a midground-to-background plant in tanks at least 60 cm long with a minimum volume of around 80 liters. Water parameters of 22–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, and soft to moderately hard water (roughly 2–15 dGH) are broadly cited, with some guides suggesting the lower end of the pH range, around 5.5–7.0, may be preferred.

The defining husbandry challenge is light and CO2. Golden Nesea can survive at moderate light without CO2 injection, but the golden-yellow leaf color and pink-to-red stems that give the plant its appeal develop reliably only under high light (typically an 8–10 hour photoperiod) combined with pressurized CO2 at around 20–30 mg/L, and consistent iron and micronutrient dosing alongside macronutrients. Under lower light conditions the plant tends to shift toward dull green and commonly drops its lower leaves. A nutrient-rich substrate — aquasoil or root tabs — is strongly recommended to support root development, and under optimal high-tech conditions the plant has been reported to grow up to around 5 cm per week.

Like other stem plants in the Lythraceae family, Golden Nesea is sensitive to sudden parameter swings. Tissue-culture specimens may melt during the transition from emersed (above-water) to submersed growth as the plant adapts to the aquarium environment; this is a normal adjustment process and the plant typically recovers once stable conditions are established.
breedingNotes: Golden Nesea is propagated in the aquarium by stem cuttings. Healthy stem sections of around 5 to 10 cm, each containing multiple nodes, are taken from the upper portion of a mature stem. The lower leaves are stripped from the cut end before the cutting is replanted in a nutrient-rich substrate. Trimming the growing tip of an established stem encourages the mother plant to produce bushier lateral side shoots rather than continuing to extend in a single column. Seed propagation is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Used for scientific name (Nesaea pedicellata), West African origin, adult height (12 in / ~30 cm or more), moderate lighting baseline, CO2 not required but beneficial, moderate growth rate, nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, midground-to-background placement, easy-to-moderate care level. Primary role assigned to Modern Aquarium because the Buce Plant URL supplied in the manifest (/products/nesaea-golden) returned HTTP 404; the live Buce listing at /products/nesaea-pedicellata-golden was instead used as a cross-reference via search snippets.

additional.name: HYGRONATURE — Ammannia pedicellata / Golden Nesaea Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked Ammannia pedicellata synonymy, pH 6.0-7.5, hardness 2-10 dKH, high light (2 W/gal minimum, 8-10 h photoperiod), CO2 optional but beneficial at 20-30 ppm, fast growth (up to ~5 cm/week), stem-cutting propagation, nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, iron-rich micronutrient dosing for golden coloration.

additional.name: Enchanted Grow — Nesaea Pedicellata (Golden)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature 22-28 C, pH 5.5-7.0 (lower than other sources), GH 2-12 dGH, high-intensity lighting 8-10 h/day, CO2 20-30 mg/L recommended, background/midground placement, moderate-to-fast growth, stem-cutting propagation with multiple nodes.

additional.name: GBIF — Ammannia pedicellata taxonomy
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy: family Lythraceae, order Myrtales; confirms Nesaea pedicellata Hiern is a synonym of Ammannia pedicellata (Hiern) S.A.Graham & Gandhi (2013 revision folding Nesaea into Ammannia).
